annotations.label: Coordinate system

anchor.snippet: a coordinate system is a system that uses one or more numbers

annotations.note: Mathlib has linear-algebraic `Basis` and manifold-theoretic `ChartedSpace`, but no general abstract definition of `coordinate system`.

annotations.label: Number line / coordinate of a point on a line

anchor.snippet: The coordinate of a point P is defined as the signed distance from O to P

annotations.note: The real line `ℝ` is pervasively used but the geometric `signed distance to origin = coordinate` framing is not formalized.

annotations.label: Cartesian coordinate system

anchor.snippet: two perpendicular lines are chosen and the coordinates of a point are taken to be the signed distances to the lines

annotations.note: `EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin n)` realises the n-tuple model and `OrthonormalBasis` gives orthonormal axes, but Mathlib has no named `Cartesian coordinate system` definition.

annotations.label: Cartesian coordinates in three dimensions

anchor.snippet: three mutually orthogonal planes are chosen and the three coordinates of a point are the signed distances to each of the planes

annotations.note: Covered as the n=3 instance of `EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin 3)` via orthonormal bases, not as a separate definition.

annotations.label: Polar coordinate system

anchor.snippet: A point is chosen as the pole and a ray from this point is taken as the polar axis

annotations.note: Defined as the open partial homeomorphism `polarCoord : OpenPartialHomeomorph (ℝ × ℝ) (ℝ × ℝ)`.

annotations.label: Non-uniqueness of polar coordinates

anchor.snippet: are all polar coordinates for the same point

annotations.note: Mathlib's `polarCoord` chooses a single branch on `(-π, π)`, so the multivalued non-uniqueness statement is not stated.

annotations.label: Cylindrical coordinate system

anchor.snippet: a z -coordinate with the same meaning as in Cartesian coordinates is added to the r and θ polar coordinates

annotations.note: No cylindrical coordinate chart found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Spherical coordinate system

anchor.snippet: Spherical coordinates take this a step further by converting the pair of cylindrical coordinates

annotations.note: No spherical coordinate chart found in Mathlib (only Euclidean spheres as sets).

annotations.label: Homogeneous coordinates

anchor.snippet: A point in the plane may be represented in homogeneous coordinates by a triple

annotations.note: Projective space exists abstractly but homogeneous coordinates as a coordinate system are not formalized.

annotations.label: Homogeneous coordinate system (general)

anchor.snippet: a homogeneous coordinate system is one where only the ratios of the coordinates are significant

annotations.note: No abstract `homogeneous coordinate system` definition found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Curvilinear coordinates

anchor.snippet: Curvilinear coordinates are a generalization of coordinate systems generally

annotations.note: No `Curvilinear` definition found in Mathlib (only `CurveIntegral` unrelatedly).

annotations.label: Orthogonal coordinates

anchor.snippet: Orthogonal coordinates : coordinate surfaces meet at right angles

annotations.note: No orthogonal coordinate system concept found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Skew coordinates

anchor.snippet: Skew coordinates : coordinate surfaces are not orthogonal

annotations.note: Not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Log-polar coordinates

anchor.snippet: The log-polar coordinate system represents a point in the plane by the logarithm of the distance from the origin

annotations.note: Grep for `logPolar`/`log-polar` returns no results.

annotations.label: Plücker coordinates

anchor.snippet: Plücker coordinates are a way of representing lines in 3D Euclidean space

annotations.note: No Plücker coordinates in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Generalized coordinates

anchor.snippet: Generalized coordinates are used in the Lagrangian treatment of mechanics

annotations.note: Lagrangian mechanics not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Canonical coordinates

anchor.snippet: Canonical coordinates are used in the Hamiltonian treatment of mechanics

annotations.note: Symplectic algebra basics exist but `canonical coordinates` are not formalized.

annotations.label: Barycentric coordinate system

anchor.snippet: Barycentric coordinate system as used for ternary plots

annotations.note: `AffineBasis.coord` gives the barycentric coordinate of a point with respect to an affine basis.

annotations.label: Trilinear coordinates

anchor.snippet: Trilinear coordinates are used in the context of triangles

annotations.note: Mathlib only has `trilinear` as in trilinear maps, not the triangle-geometry coordinate system.

annotations.label: Whewell equation

anchor.snippet: The Whewell equation relates arc length and the tangential angle

annotations.note: Whewell equation not present.

annotations.label: Cesàro equation

anchor.snippet: The Cesàro equation relates arc length and curvature

annotations.note: Cesàro hits in Mathlib refer to Cesàro means/summation, not the intrinsic curve equation.

annotations.label: Line coordinates

anchor.snippet: the term line coordinates is used for any coordinate system that specifies the position of a line

annotations.note: Line coordinates as a concept are not formalized.

annotations.label: Dualistic coordinate systems / principle of duality

anchor.snippet: The two systems in a case like this are said to be dualistic

annotations.note: Projective duality is not formalized in the coordinate-geometry sense described.

annotations.label: Coordinate transformation

anchor.snippet: The relationship between different systems is described by coordinate transformations

annotations.note: Change-of-chart maps in `ChartedSpace` formalise coordinate transformations between charts; no standalone named definition.

annotations.label: Polar to Cartesian transformation

anchor.snippet: the coordinate transformation from polar to Cartesian coordinates is given by

annotations.note: `polarCoord.symm p = (p.1 * cos p.2, p.1 * sin p.2)` is exactly the polar-to-Cartesian formula.

annotations.label: Coordinate transformations from a bijection

anchor.snippet: With every bijection from the space to itself two coordinate transformations can be associated

annotations.note: Mathlib has `Equiv` for bijections but no stated correspondence between bijections and pairs of coordinate transformations.

annotations.label: 1D translation transformation

anchor.snippet: if the mapping is a translation of 3 to the right

annotations.note: Translations as homeomorphisms/equivs are available, but this specific example is not stated.

annotations.label: Coordinate curve and coordinate line

anchor.snippet: if one of the coordinates of a point varies while the other coordinates are held constant, then the resulting curve is called a coordinate curve

annotations.note: No notion of `coordinate curve` is defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Curvilinear coordinate system

anchor.snippet: A coordinate system for which some coordinate curves are not lines is called a curvilinear coordinate system

annotations.note: Not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Coordinate axis

anchor.snippet: A coordinate line with all other constant coordinates equal to zero is called a coordinate axis

annotations.note: Axes show up implicitly via basis vectors but `coordinate axis` is not a named definition.

annotations.label: Polar coordinates as curvilinear system

anchor.snippet: A polar coordinate system is a curvilinear system where coordinate curves are lines or circles

annotations.note: Mathlib does not characterise polar coordinates as `curvilinear` in this sense.

annotations.label: Parabolic coordinate curves

anchor.snippet: the coordinate curves of parabolic coordinates are parabolas

annotations.note: Parabolic coordinates not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Coordinate surface

anchor.snippet: if one coordinate is held constant and the other two are allowed to vary, then the resulting surface is called a coordinate surface

annotations.note: `coordinate surface` not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Spherical coordinate surfaces

anchor.snippet: the coordinate surfaces obtained by holding ρ constant in the spherical coordinate system are the spheres with center at the origin

annotations.note: No spherical coordinates in Mathlib, so this example is not stated.

annotations.label: Coordinate hypersurface

anchor.snippet: coordinate hypersurfaces are the

annotations.note: Not formalized.

annotations.label: Coordinate map / chart

anchor.snippet: a coordinate map is a homeomorphism from an open subset of a space X to an open subset of

annotations.note: Charts of a `ChartedSpace` are `PartialHomeomorph` from `M` to the model space, exactly matching coordinate maps.

annotations.label: Atlas and manifold

anchor.snippet: a collection of coordinate maps are put together to form an atlas covering the space

annotations.note: `ChartedSpace.atlas` is the set of charts covering the manifold, formalising atlases.

annotations.label: Differentiable manifold

anchor.snippet: a differentiable manifold is a manifold where the change of coordinates from one coordinate map to another is always a differentiable function

annotations.note: `IsManifold I n M` asserts the change-of-coordinates are `C^n`, covering the differentiable-manifold notion.

annotations.label: Orientation matrix

anchor.snippet: the orientation of a rigid body can be represented by an orientation matrix

annotations.note: `Orientation` (as a `Module.Ray` of alternating forms) captures orientations of modules, but `orientation matrix` of a rigid body is not specifically defined.

annotations.label: Geographic coordinate system

anchor.snippet: Geographic coordinate system , the spherical coordinates of latitude and longitude

annotations.note: Geographic coordinates not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Projected coordinate systems

anchor.snippet: Projected coordinate systems , including thousands of cartesian coordinate systems

annotations.note: Not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Geocentric coordinate system

anchor.snippet: Geocentric coordinate system , a three-dimensional cartesian coordinate system that models the earth as an object

annotations.note: Not formalized in Mathlib.
